title: Bucić–Sudakov–Tran Conjecture (multidimensional Erdős–Szekeres)
statement_text: A doubly exponential upper bound holds for the multidimensional Erdős–Szekeres numbers $M_d(n)$ in every dimension $d$.
context_text: Fishburn and Graham proved a multidimensional Erdős–Szekeres theorem in 1993, and Bucić, Sudakov, and Tran later established doubly exponential upper bounds for $d=2,3$ while conjecturing such bounds extend to all dimensions. The present paper's Theorem 1.4 provides a doubly exponential upper bound for $d\geq 4$, improving on the previous triply exponential upper bound of Bucić–Sudakov–Tran.
paper_contribution: Confirms the conjecture by proving a doubly exponential upper bound on $M_d(n)$ for all $d\geq 4$, improving the previously known triply exponential upper bound.
notes: Exact LaTeX statement not provided in the supplied paper content; statement reconstructed from context in abstract and Section 4. The conjecture is attributed to arXiv:1910.13318 (Bucić, Sudakov, Tran).

title: Problem 4.1
context_text: Theorem 1.1 gives a doubly exponential upper bound on the $d$-dimensional Ramsey numbers $R_r(d,n)$, while the best known lower bound is only singly exponential (from a random colouring argument). Closing this gap—determining whether a simple exponential upper bound suffices or the true growth is faster—is identified as a key open problem.

title: Problem 4.2
statement_text: Does there exist $C>0$ such that $\log R_{2}(2,n)\leq(\log(M_{2}(n))^{C}$?
context_text: Since $M_2(n)\leq R_2(2,n)$ for every $n$, the authors ask whether the two-dimensional Ramsey number $R_2(2,n)$ can be bounded above by a fixed power of $\log M_2(n)$, where $M_2(n)$ is the two-dimensional Erdős–Szekeres number. A singly-to-doubly exponential gap persists for both quantities.
notes: The statement as supplied appears to have a missing closing parenthesis: $(\log(M_{2}(n))^{C}$ is reproduced verbatim from the source.
